8	---
9	# Solution Design: Task Execution Lifecycle
10	
11	## Purpose
12	
13	This design turns FEAT-010's task execution lifecycle into an implementation
14	shape. It is intentionally below the feature spec and above code-level storage
15	types: enough detail to keep the `run`, `try`, and `work` layers aligned
16	without re-opening the routing boundary in FEAT-006.
17	
18	## Layer Model
19	
20	Task execution is composed from named layers:
21	
22	| Layer | Public verb | Meaning |
23	| --- | --- | --- |
24	| 1 | `ddx run` | one upstream agent invocation |
25	| 2 | `ddx try <bead>` | one bead attempt in an isolated worktree |
26	| 3 | `ddx work` | one mechanical queue-drain loop |
27	
28	Each higher layer references child records from the layer beneath it. Comparison,
29	replay, benchmark, review, and artifact-regeneration workflows are compositions
30	over the task execution lifecycle, not new run kinds.
31	
32	## Record Shape
33	
34	Every record lives under `.ddx/runs/<run-id>/record.json`. Large bodies are
35	attachments in the same directory.
36	
37	```json
38	{
39	  "run_id": "run_20260430T120102Z_01HV...",
40	  "layer": 2,
41	  "parent_run_id": "run_20260430T115900Z_01HV...",
42	  "started_at": "2026-04-30T12:01:02Z",
43	  "finished_at": "2026-04-30T12:04:12Z",
44	  "terminal_status": "success",
45	  "actor": "erik",
46	  "host": "sindri",
47	  "git_revision": "abc123",
48	  "ddx_version": "0.0.0-dev",
49	  "produces_artifact": null,
50	  "attachments": [
51	    {"name": "prompt", "path": "prompt.md", "media_type": "text/markdown"},
52	    {"name": "result", "path": "result.json", "media_type": "application/json"}
53	  ],
54	  "layer1": null,
55	  "layer2": {
56	    "bead_id": "ddx-12345678",
57	    "base_revision": "abc123",
58	    "result_revision": "def456",
59	    "worktree_path": ".ddx/worktrees/ddx-12345678-run_...",
60	    "finalization": "merge",
61	    "child_run_ids": ["run_20260430T120104Z_01HV..."],
62	    "gate_summary": "go test ./internal/agent"
63	  },
64	  "layer3": null
65	}
66	```
67	
68	Rules:
69	
70	- `layer` is the discriminator; exactly one of `layer1`, `layer2`, or `layer3`
71	  is populated.
72	- `parent_run_id` points upward when a run is nested; child ids live in the
73	  layer extension for efficient drill-down.
74	- `terminal_status` is the substrate outcome. Domain-specific detail remains in
75	  the layer extension and attachments.
76	- `produces_artifact` is set when a run regenerates an artifact; FEAT-007 reads
77	  it as provenance for `generated_by`.
78	- Records are append-only. Correction means writing a new record or a separate
79	  review/annotation artifact, not editing a historical record.
80	
81	## Layer Extensions
82	
83	### Layer 1: invocation atom
84	
85	Layer 1 stores the DDx request envelope and the agent's typed response fields:
86	
87	- prompt attachment or prompt reference
88	- requested `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower`
89	- opaque passthrough constraints (`harness`, `provider`, `model`) when supplied
90	- non-routing work facts such as permissions, effort, timeout, and artifact id
91	- actual model and actual power returned by the agent
92	- token/cost/duration metadata
93	- upstream session id and session-log attachment pointer
94	- structured result attachment
95	
96	DDx does not persist route decisions as DDx-owned policy. Concrete
97	harness/provider/model values are recorded only as requested passthrough or
98	agent-reported facts.
99	
100	### Layer 2: bead attempt
101	
102	Layer 2 stores DDx-owned attempt orchestration:
103	
104	- bead id and governing artifact ids
105	- base revision and worktree path
106	- child layer-1 run ids
107	- commit/result revision, if produced
108	- finalization mode: `merge`, `preserve`, `no_changes`, or `aborted`
109	- preserve ref, if any
110	- gate results and review verdicts
111	- evidence-bundle attachment refs
112	- cleanup summary: removed worktree path, preserve reason, partial setup
113	  cleanup, and resource-preflight result
114	
115	Layer 2 owns success classification for a bead attempt because DDx owns commits,
116	merge/preserve state, no-change rationale, post-run gates, and review evidence.
117	The agent's exit status and actual power are inputs, not the full decision.
118	Per FEAT-010's network-free drain boundary, this layer performs no network I/O;
119	origin-sync and push are out-of-band per FEAT-023.
120	
121	### Layer 3: queue drain
122	
123	Layer 3 stores the mechanical queue loop:
124	
125	- queue snapshot at start
126	- child layer-2 attempt ids in execution order
127	- retry/escalation decisions with requested `MinPower` changes
128	- no-progress counter state
129	- stop-condition evaluation log
130	- cleanup pass summaries and resource stop details
131	- terminal disposition: `drained`, `blocked`, `deferred`, `no_progress`,
132	  `signal`, or `resource_exhausted`
133	
134	Layer 3 does not file new content-aware work or decide workflow policy.
135	Supervisory behavior belongs to skills or plugins layered on top.
136	Per FEAT-010's network-free drain boundary, this layer performs no network I/O;
137	origin-sync and push are out-of-band per FEAT-023.
138	
139	## On-Disk Layout
140	
141	```text
142	.ddx/
143	└── runs/
144	    └── <run-id>/
145	        ├── record.json
146	        ├── prompt.md
147	        ├── result.json
148	        ├── session.jsonl
149	        ├── stdout.log
150	        ├── stderr.log
151	        └── evidence/
152	            ├── manifest.json
153	            └── checks.json
154	```
155	
156	Writers create records in a temporary directory and atomically rename into
157	`.ddx/runs/<run-id>/`. A partially written directory is never published as a
158	complete run. Attachment paths in `record.json` are relative to the run
159	directory.
160	
161	## Legacy Migration
162	
163	Two existing layouts are read during the migration window:
164	
165	- `.ddx/exec-runs/` or `.ddx/exec-runs.d/` records become virtual layer-1
166	  records.
167	- `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>/` bundles become virtual layer-2 records.
168	
169	Migration rules:
170	
171	1. New writes always target `.ddx/runs/`.
172	2. Readers expose legacy records through the same `ddx runs` query model.
173	3. A one-shot rewrite can normalize legacy records into `.ddx/runs/`.
174	4. After the documented cutoff, fallback readers are removed.
175	
176	The migration must preserve git-tracked execute-bead evidence. It must not
177	rewrite historical bead attempt commits or `closing_commit_sha` pointers.
178	
179	## Layer 3.5: Auto-Recovery
180	
181	Auto-recovery is a distinct stage that runs **between** Layer 3 (queue drain) and the `status=proposed` operator-review escape. It is triggered when a bead's within-cycle escalation ladder has been exhausted on two or more consecutive drain cycles (`Extra["consecutive_ladder_exhaustions"] >= 2`; see TD-031 §6 `consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` field). The bead must be `status=open` and not carry the `recovery:manual` label.
182	
183	### Trigger
184	
185	The drain loop evaluates the auto-recovery trigger after each drain cycle before selecting the next candidate. If the trigger condition is met for any `status=open`, execution-eligible bead, the loop claims the bead (or waits for the existing claim to release) and enters the auto-recovery sequence. The trigger does not fire during a running attempt; it fires only between attempts.
186	
187	### Sequence
188	
189	1. **Reframe attempt** — dispatch a strong-tier reframer agent (ADR-024 P3; TD-031 §6 Role Catalogue) with `MinPower` set above the exhausted escalation ceiling. The reframer receives the bead record (description, AC, governing artifact refs) and returns structured edits. If the reframer returns edits, DDx applies them via `ddx bead update` paths, records `reframe-applied` (TD-031 §4), resets `consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` to 0, releases the claim, and returns the bead to `status=open` execution-ready. The bead re-enters the standard drain cycle with a fresh ladder.
190	2. **Decompose attempt** (only when reframe returned no change or the reframer invocation failed) — dispatch a strong-tier decomposer agent with the same `MinPower` floor. The decomposer returns 2–5 child bead specs. If child specs are returned, DDx calls `Store.Create` for each child, wires dependency edges from each child to the parent, sets `Extra["execution-eligible"]=false` on the parent, and records `decompose-applied` (TD-031 §4). The parent remains `status=open` but is now dependency-waiting on its children.
191	3. **Final escape** (only when the decomposer returned no children or failed) — DDx records `auto-recovery-failed` (TD-031 §4), moves the bead to `status=proposed`, clears the active claim, and does not schedule another attempt. The operator must resolve the bead via `ddx bead update --status open` (after fixing it) or `ddx bead update --status cancelled`.
192	
193	### Layer-3 record
194	
195	The auto-recovery sequence runs within the layer-3 run record as an additional stop-condition evaluation entry. The entry names the bead, the trigger condition (`consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` value), which recovery step was attempted, the outcome, and the cost of any reframer/decomposer invocations. These costs contribute to the drain-level budget cap (FEAT-014).
196	
197	### Cross-references
198	
199	- **TD-031 §5** — `reframe_applied`, `decompose_applied`, `auto_recovery_failed`, and `per_bead_budget_exhausted` outcome rows define the status transitions and Extra mutations.
200	- **ADR-024 P4** — the principle authorizing this stage.
201	- **ADR-024 Escalation Sequencing** — the strict ordering of within-cycle, cross-cycle, and `status=proposed` escapes.
202	- **TD-031 §6 Role Catalogue** — reframer and decomposer dispatch contracts.
203	- **TD-031 §6 `consecutive_ladder_exhaustions`** — the counter that drives the trigger.
204	
205	## Stop Conditions
206	
207	`ddx work` evaluates stop conditions only between `ddx try` attempts:
208	
209	| Disposition | Trigger |
210	| --- | --- |
211	| `drained` | no ready beads remain |
212	| `blocked` | remaining ready beads are terminal for the current policy |
213	| `deferred` | wall-clock or attempt-count budget exhausted |
214	| `no_progress` | configured consecutive attempts produced no commit and no merged side effect |
215	| `signal` | SIGINT/SIGTERM received; first signal cancels in-flight work cooperatively and stops new claims |
216	| `resource_exhausted` | execution roots are not safely writable or lack required bytes/inodes after cleanup |
217	
218	The layer-3 evaluation log records the candidate bead, previous outcome,
219	retry eligibility, requested power bounds, passthrough envelope presence, and
220	the condition that fired. The log may inspect DDx-owned attempt outcomes and
221	agent typed status; it must not branch on concrete provider/model identity.
222	
223	Signal handling is process-root behavior, not a per-command special case.
224	The root command installs a SIGINT/SIGTERM-backed context. On the first signal,
225	DDx writes `Cancel received, shutting down gracefully` to stderr/stdout visible
226	to the operator, cancels the root context, and lets `ddx work` / `ddx try`
227	finish the TD-031 interruption path: preserve available evidence, remove
228	runtime liveness state, release any non-terminal bead claim, and exit with
229	layer-3 disposition `signal`. A second signal may hard-abort rather than wait
230	for cleanup.
231	
232	## Cleanup Manager
233	
234	The run substrate includes one DDx-owned cleanup manager used by `ddx try`,
235	`ddx work`, server-managed workers, and an explicit operator cleanup command.
236	The manager is scoped to DDx-created execution resources only; it never scans or
237	removes arbitrary temp directories.
238	
239	### Roots and ownership
240	
241	The manager knows the configured temporary worktree root, durable run/evidence
242	roots, worker liveness root, and project git repository. Every created
243	temporary execution directory records enough ownership to answer:
244	
245	- which project created it
246	- which attempt or worker owns it
247	- whether the attempt reached published evidence
248	- whether it was intentionally preserved
249	- when its liveness signal was last refreshed
250	
251	During the legacy migration window, the manager handles both
252	`.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>` and `.ddx/runs/<run-id>` evidence, but it treats
253	complete evidence bundles as durable data rather than scratch.
254	DDx-owned cleanup scope also includes DDx-created test and e2e scratch roots,
255	generated test binaries, and run-state or liveness files. Recognized DDx-owned
256	scratch prefixes are: `ddx-exec-wt`, `ddx-claim-heartbeats`,
257	`ddx-metric-keepalive`, `ddx-test-`, and `ddx-e2e-`. Any directory containing
258	a `cleanup.json` metadata file with matching project ownership is DDx-owned
259	regardless of its name. A path is eligible for deletion when: (a) it has
260	`cleanup.json` metadata and the liveness is expired or the owning attempt is
261	terminal; or (b) it matches a recognized DDx prefix without metadata, the
262	directory mtime is at least **6 hours** old, and no live PID or active session
263	is present. The manager preserves published evidence and registered active
264	worktrees.
265	
266	### Entry points
267	
268	Inline cleanup runs inside `ddx try` before and after worktree lifecycle
269	operations. It removes partial directories from failed setup and removes the
270	isolated worktree after merge, preserve, no-changes, no-evidence, failed
271	publish, or cooperative interruption finalization.
272	
273	Loop cleanup runs inside `ddx work` at startup, between attempts after
274	setup/finalization failure, periodically while polling, and during graceful
275	shutdown. It may clean stale resources from prior attempts before the next
276	claim.
277	
278	Background cleanup runs occasionally while long-lived DDx processes are alive.
279	It uses jitter plus a project cleanup lock so concurrent workers do not all
280	prune at once. It must tolerate another process winning the race to remove a
281	resource.
282	
283	An explicit operator command, such as `ddx cleanup` or `ddx doctor cleanup`,
284	runs the same manager and reports what it removed without requiring a queue
285	drain.
286	
287	### Conservative deletion rules
288	
289	The manager may delete:
290	
291	- unregistered directories under DDx temp worktree roots whose names and
292	  metadata identify DDx ownership and whose liveness is absent or stale
293	- registered git worktrees under DDx temp roots whose owning attempt is
294	  terminal or whose liveness marker is stale
295	- DDx-created test and e2e scratch roots that satisfy the same ownership,
296	  age, and liveness rules
297	- generated test binaries and run-state or liveness files that are DDx-owned
298	- stale heartbeat/liveness files for dead PIDs or expired sessions
299	- partial setup directories that never reached atomic evidence publication
300	- old non-preserved scratch data past configured retention
301	
302	The manager must not delete:
303	
304	- preserved attempt worktrees
305	- local evidence refs under `refs/ddx/iterations/...`
306	- complete `.ddx/runs/<id>` or `.ddx/executions/<attempt-id>` evidence
307	- active worktrees with a live PID/session heartbeat
308	- paths outside configured DDx roots
309	- paths that only loosely resemble DDx names without matching ownership
310	  metadata or registered worktree evidence
311	
312	### Resource preflight and loop-fatal handling
313	
314	Before `ddx try` claims a bead or creates a worktree, it checks the configured
315	temp worktree root and durable evidence root for writability, free bytes, and
316	free inodes when inode data is available. If preflight fails, `ddx try` runs one
317	immediate cleanup pass and repeats the check. If the check still fails, the
318	attempt returns `resource_exhausted` without claiming the bead.
319	
320	`ddx work` and server-managed workers perform the same cleanup pass before the
321	first claim and before any later claim when any checked temp or evidence root
322	falls below the soft cleanup trigger of **512 MiB free bytes** or **8192 free
323	inodes**. If the temp roots remain below the hard stop floor of **64 MiB free
324	bytes** and **1024 free inodes** after that cleanup, the loop records host
325	exhaustion and stops claiming new beads.
326	
327	If resource exhaustion occurs after a claim or during worktree setup,
328	`ddx try` records whatever partial evidence is available, removes any partial
329	unregistered directory it created, releases the claim if the bead did not close,
330	and returns `resource_exhausted`.
331	
332	`ddx work` treats `resource_exhausted` as host infrastructure failure. It
333	records a layer-3 stop-condition entry, prints an operator-visible message such
334	as `resource exhausted after cleanup; stopping work loop`, and stops claiming
335	new beads. It must not continue scanning the ready queue in the same process.
336	
337	Cleanup output is structured. Routine passes are trace/debug or worker events.
338	Passes that reclaim meaningful bytes or inodes emit an operator-visible summary
339	including counts. Failures include path, class, and whether the failure blocked
340	progress.
341	
342	Expected implementation tests include `TestExecutionCleanup_RemovesStaleDDXScratchDirs`,
343	`TestWorkResourcePreflight_RunsCleanupBelowSoftFloor`, and
344	`TestWorkResourcePreflight_StopsBelowHardFloorAfterCleanup`.
345	
346	## Drill-Down Query Model
347	
348	The read API is centered on `ddx runs`:
349	
350	- `ddx runs list --layer 1|2|3 --bead <id> --artifact <id>`
351	- `ddx runs show <run-id>`
352	- `ddx runs children <run-id>`
353	- `ddx runs parents <run-id>`
354	- `ddx runs log <run-id>`
355	- `ddx runs result <run-id>`
356	
357	Server and MCP surfaces mirror those reads. A layer-3 detail page drills down
358	to child layer-2 attempts; each layer-2 attempt drills down to child layer-1
359	invocations. The graph is navigated by `parent_run_id` and child id arrays, not
360	by path guessing.
361	
362	## Implementation Notes
363	
364	- Reuse the existing execution attachment discipline where it still fits, but
365	  do not keep `.ddx/exec-runs` and `.ddx/executions` as peer authoritative
366	  writers.
367	- Keep passthrough constraints in one request/record subobject so harness,
368	  provider, and model do not spread through queue, retry, or scheduling code.
369	- Treat artifact regeneration as metadata on layer 1 or layer 2, depending on
370	  whether the generator returns content directly or edits the repo in a
371	  worktree.
372	- Add tests at the storage boundary for atomic publish, legacy read
373	  compatibility, parent/child traversal, and no-progress stop-condition
374	  recording.

1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"crypto/rand"
6		"encoding/hex"
7		"fmt"
8		"path/filepath"
9		"sort"
10		"strings"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/checks"
15		internalgit "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
16	)
17	
18	// PreMergeChecksDir is the project-relative directory scanned for check
19	// definitions before each land-back attempt.
20	const PreMergeChecksDir = ".ddx/checks"
21	
22	// PreMergeChecksEvidenceSubdir is the per-attempt subdirectory under the
23	// execution evidence dir into which check result files (one JSON per check)
24	// are written.
25	const PreMergeChecksEvidenceSubdir = "checks"
26	
27	// PreMergeChecksOutcome captures everything callers need to (a) decide
28	// whether to land or preserve and (b) record events on the bead.
29	type PreMergeChecksOutcome struct {
30		// Results is the per-check outcome for every check that actually ran
31		// (post-AppliesTo filter, post-bypass filter). Empty when no checks
32		// applied.
33		Results []checks.Result
34		// Bypassed lists checks_bypass entries that took effect (i.e. matched a
35		// loaded check by name and were honoured for this run). Each entry is
36		// recorded as a `checks-bypass` event by AppendPreMergeChecksEvents.
37		Bypassed []bead.ChecksBypassEntry
38		// Blocked is true when at least one applicable, non-bypassed check
39		// returned status=block or status=error. The caller MUST NOT land the
40		// worker's result when Blocked is true; PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks
41		// performs the canonical preservation + event-record dance.
42		Blocked bool
43		// BlockingNames lists the names of the checks responsible for Blocked=true,
44		// sorted alphabetically for stable ordering in evidence.
45		BlockingNames []string
46		// Reason is a short, human-readable summary suitable for the
47		// ExecuteBeadResult.Reason / land Reason field on a preserved outcome.
48		Reason string
49		// EvidenceDir is the absolute directory where per-check JSON result files
50		// were written (the directory the checks runner used as EVIDENCE_DIR).
51		EvidenceDir string
52		// AttemptID/BaseRev/ResultRev identify the worker attempt whose candidate
53		// was evaluated.
54		AttemptID string
55		BaseRev   string
56		ResultRev string
57	}
58	
59	// RunPreMergeChecks loads all .ddx/checks/*.yaml definitions for the project,
60	// filters by AppliesTo against the bead's labels and the changed-paths set
61	// computed from base..result, removes any checks named in the bead's
62	// checks_bypass annotation, and runs the rest in parallel via checks.Run.
63	//
64	// The bead's checks_bypass annotation is validated up front via
65	// bead.ChecksBypasses; any malformed entry (missing reason, etc.) returns a
66	// non-nil error so the caller can preserve loudly instead of silently
67	// honouring an invalid bypass.
68	//
69	// evidenceDir is the absolute path to the per-attempt execution evidence
70	// directory (typically <projectRoot>/.ddx/executions/<run-id>/). Check result
71	// files are written under evidenceDir/checks/. The directory is created if it
72	// does not exist.
73	//
74	// When the project has no .ddx/checks/ directory or no applicable checks,
75	// the returned outcome has Blocked=false and empty Results — the caller
76	// should proceed with the land as if no gate were configured.
77	func RunPreMergeChecks(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, b *bead.Bead, baseRev, resultRev, evidenceDir string) (*PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
78		if projectRoot == "" {
79			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: project_root required")
80		}
81		if b == nil {
82			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: bead required")
83		}
84	
85		bypasses, err := bead.ChecksBypasses(b)
86		if err != nil {
87			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: invalid checks_bypass annotation: %w", err)
88		}
89	
90		all, err := checks.LoadDir(filepath.Join(projectRoot, PreMergeChecksDir))
91		if err != nil {
92			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: load: %w", err)
93		}
94		if len(all) == 0 {
95			return &PreMergeChecksOutcome{}, nil
96		}
97	
98		bypassByName := make(map[string]bead.ChecksBypassEntry, len(bypasses))
99		for _, e := range bypasses {
100			bypassByName[e.Name] = e
101		}
102	
103		var toRun []checks.Check
104		var honouredBypass []bead.ChecksBypassEntry
105		for _, c := range all {
106			if e, ok := bypassByName[c.Name]; ok {
107				honouredBypass = append(honouredBypass, e)
108				continue
109			}
110			toRun = append(toRun, c)

### SUMMARY OF IDENTIFIER PATTERNS FOUND

| Pattern | Files Using | Primary Use |
|---------|-------------|------------|
| `runID` (camelCase) | agent, exec, checks, metric, server GraphQL | Go struct fields, local variables |
| `RunID` (PascalCase) | exec/types.go, metric/types.go, checks/protocol.go | Struct field definitions |
| `run_id` (snake_case) | JSON fields, YAML fields, audit logs | Serialization and data formats |
| `run-id` (kebab-case) | Comments, CLI help text, path documentation | User-facing strings and documentation |
| `parent_run_id` / `ParentRunID` | server state/runs | Parent execution tracking |
| `child_run_ids` / `ChildRunIDs` | server state/runs, SD-025 schema | Child execution tracking |

---

### ARCHITECTURAL CONTEXT

From the spec documents, the run-id system serves:

1. **Layer 1 (Agent Invocation)**: Identifies individual upstream agent invocations
2. **Layer 2 (Bead Attempts)**: Tracks worktree-isolated bead execution attempts  
3. **Layer 3 (Queue Drain)**: Records mechanical queue-loop sessions
4. **Evidence Assembly**: Each run record lives in `.ddx/runs/<run-id>/record.json` with attachments
5. **Cost Tracking**: RunID correlates with usage and cost evidence (FEAT-014)
6. **Audit Trail**: RunID appears in audit logs with correlation metadata (role, bead_id, attempt_id, session_id)

The identifiers are stable across retry, escalation, and decomposition cycles, enabling deterministic proof of work and cost tracking.

1	// Package checks implements the language-agnostic pre-merge check protocol.
2	//
3	// A check is a project-declared mechanical gate evaluated before a worker's
4	// worktree is merged back. Each check is one YAML file under
5	// .ddx/checks/*.yaml describing a command to run and the situations it
6	// applies to (path globs, bead labels). The check command is invoked as a
7	// subprocess with the following environment variables injected:
8	//
9	//	BEAD_ID       — the bead being evaluated
10	//	DIFF_BASE     — git revision the work started from
11	//	DIFF_HEAD     — current worktree HEAD
12	//	PROJECT_ROOT  — absolute path to the project root
13	//	EVIDENCE_DIR  — directory the check must write its result file into
14	//	RUN_ID        — unique identifier for this evaluation run
15	//
16	// The check writes its result to ${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${name}.json with shape:
17	//
18	//	{
19	//	  "status":   "pass" | "block" | "error",
20	//	  "message":  "<human-readable summary>",
21	//	  "violations": [
22	//	    {"file": "...", "line": 0, "symbol": "...", "kind": "...", "detail": "..."}
23	//	  ]
24	//	}
25	//
26	// Exit code 0 indicates the check ran to completion (the status field in the
27	// result file is authoritative). A non-zero exit, a missing result file, or
28	// an unparseable result file is treated as status=error.
29	package checks
30	
31	// Status enumerates the three possible check outcomes.
32	type Status string
33	
34	const (
35		StatusPass  Status = "pass"
36		StatusBlock Status = "block"
37		StatusError Status = "error"
38	)
39	
40	// HookPreMerge is the only currently supported lifecycle hook.
41	const HookPreMerge = "pre_merge"
42	
43	// Check is the parsed representation of one .ddx/checks/*.yaml file.
44	type Check struct {
45		// Name uniquely identifies the check; also the basename of its result
46		// file (${EVIDENCE_DIR}/${Name}.json). Required.
47		Name string `yaml:"name"`
48		// Command is the shell command to execute. Required. Run via
49		// `sh -c <command>` so projects can use pipes/redirection.
50		Command string `yaml:"command"`
51		// When is the lifecycle hook the check binds to. Currently only
52		// "pre_merge" is supported. Required.
53		When string `yaml:"when"`
54		// AppliesTo gates whether this check runs for a given (paths, labels)
55		// invocation context. Zero value means "always".
56		AppliesTo AppliesTo `yaml:"applies_to"`
57	
58		// SourceFile records the on-disk path the check was loaded from
59		// (used for diagnostics). Not part of the YAML schema.
60		SourceFile string `yaml:"-"`
61	}
62	
63	// AppliesTo declares the conditions under which a check is evaluated.
64	//
65	// Semantics:
66	//   - If neither Paths nor Labels is set, the check ALWAYS applies.
67	//   - If Paths is set, the check applies when any changed path matches
68	//     any glob, evaluated against slash-normalized paths relative to
69	//     PROJECT_ROOT. The recursive "**" segment is supported.
70	//   - If Labels is set, the check applies when the bead carries any of
71	//     the listed labels.
72	//   - If both are set, EITHER a matching path OR a matching label causes
73	//     the check to apply (logical OR).
74	type AppliesTo struct {
75		Paths  []string `yaml:"paths,omitempty"`
76		Labels []string `yaml:"labels,omitempty"`
77	}
78	
79	// Result is the parsed outcome of running one check.
80	type Result struct {
81		Name       string      `json:"name"`
82		Status     Status      `json:"status"`
83		Message    string      `json:"message,omitempty"`
84		Violations []Violation `json:"violations,omitempty"`
85	
86		// ExitCode and Stderr are captured by the runner for diagnostics
87		// when status=error. Not part of the on-disk JSON contract.
88		ExitCode int    `json:"-"`
89		Stderr   string `json:"-"`
90	}
91	
92	// Violation describes one offending location reported by a check.
93	type Violation struct {
94		File   string `json:"file,omitempty"`
95		Line   int    `json:"line,omitempty"`
96		Symbol string `json:"symbol,omitempty"`
97		Kind   string `json:"kind,omitempty"`
98		Detail string `json:"detail,omitempty"`
99	}
100	
101	// InvocationContext bundles the runtime values injected as environment
102	// variables into each check command, plus the bead labels and changed
103	// paths used to evaluate AppliesTo filtering.
104	type InvocationContext struct {
105		BeadID       string
106		DiffBase     string
107		DiffHead     string
108		ProjectRoot  string
109		EvidenceDir  string
110		RunID        string
111		BeadLabels   []string
112		ChangedPaths []string
113	}

14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/checks"
15		internalgit "github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/git"
16	)
17	
18	// PreMergeChecksDir is the project-relative directory scanned for check
19	// definitions before each land-back attempt.
20	const PreMergeChecksDir = ".ddx/checks"
21	
22	// PreMergeChecksEvidenceSubdir is the per-attempt subdirectory under the
23	// execution evidence dir into which check result files (one JSON per check)
24	// are written.
25	const PreMergeChecksEvidenceSubdir = "checks"
26	
27	// PreMergeChecksOutcome captures everything callers need to (a) decide
28	// whether to land or preserve and (b) record events on the bead.
29	type PreMergeChecksOutcome struct {
30		// Results is the per-check outcome for every check that actually ran
31		// (post-AppliesTo filter, post-bypass filter). Empty when no checks
32		// applied.
33		Results []checks.Result
34		// Bypassed lists checks_bypass entries that took effect (i.e. matched a
35		// loaded check by name and were honoured for this run). Each entry is
36		// recorded as a `checks-bypass` event by AppendPreMergeChecksEvents.
37		Bypassed []bead.ChecksBypassEntry
38		// Blocked is true when at least one applicable, non-bypassed check
39		// returned status=block or status=error. The caller MUST NOT land the
40		// worker's result when Blocked is true; PreserveAfterPreMergeChecks
41		// performs the canonical preservation + event-record dance.
42		Blocked bool
43		// BlockingNames lists the names of the checks responsible for Blocked=true,
44		// sorted alphabetically for stable ordering in evidence.
45		BlockingNames []string
46		// Reason is a short, human-readable summary suitable for the
47		// ExecuteBeadResult.Reason / land Reason field on a preserved outcome.
48		Reason string
49		// EvidenceDir is the absolute directory where per-check JSON result files
50		// were written (the directory the checks runner used as EVIDENCE_DIR).
51		EvidenceDir string
52		// AttemptID/BaseRev/ResultRev identify the worker attempt whose candidate
53		// was evaluated.
54		AttemptID string
55		BaseRev   string
56		ResultRev string
57	}
58	
59	// RunPreMergeChecks loads all .ddx/checks/*.yaml definitions for the project,
60	// filters by AppliesTo against the bead's labels and the changed-paths set
61	// computed from base..result, removes any checks named in the bead's
62	// checks_bypass annotation, and runs the rest in parallel via checks.Run.
63	//
64	// The bead's checks_bypass annotation is validated up front via
65	// bead.ChecksBypasses; any malformed entry (missing reason, etc.) returns a
66	// non-nil error so the caller can preserve loudly instead of silently
67	// honouring an invalid bypass.
68	//
69	// evidenceDir is the absolute path to the per-attempt execution evidence
70	// directory (typically <projectRoot>/.ddx/executions/<run-id>/). Check result
71	// files are written under evidenceDir/checks/. The directory is created if it
72	// does not exist.
73	//
74	// When the project has no .ddx/checks/ directory or no applicable checks,
75	// the returned outcome has Blocked=false and empty Results — the caller
76	// should proceed with the land as if no gate were configured.
77	func RunPreMergeChecks(ctx context.Context, projectRoot string, b *bead.Bead, baseRev, resultRev, evidenceDir string) (*PreMergeChecksOutcome, error) {
78		if projectRoot == "" {
79			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: project_root required")
80		}
81		if b == nil {
82			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: bead required")
83		}
84	
85		bypasses, err := bead.ChecksBypasses(b)
86		if err != nil {
87			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: invalid checks_bypass annotation: %w", err)
88		}
89	
90		all, err := checks.LoadDir(filepath.Join(projectRoot, PreMergeChecksDir))
91		if err != nil {
92			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: load: %w", err)
93		}
94		if len(all) == 0 {
95			return &PreMergeChecksOutcome{}, nil
96		}
97	
98		bypassByName := make(map[string]bead.ChecksBypassEntry, len(bypasses))
99		for _, e := range bypasses {
100			bypassByName[e.Name] = e
101		}
102	
103		var toRun []checks.Check
104		var honouredBypass []bead.ChecksBypassEntry
105		for _, c := range all {
106			if e, ok := bypassByName[c.Name]; ok {
107				honouredBypass = append(honouredBypass, e)
108				continue
109			}
110			toRun = append(toRun, c)
111		}
112	
113		changed, err := preMergeChangedPaths(projectRoot, baseRev, resultRev)
114		if err != nil {
115			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: diff base..result: %w", err)
116		}
117	
118		checksEvidence := filepath.Join(evidenceDir, PreMergeChecksEvidenceSubdir)
119		ictx := checks.InvocationContext{
120			BeadID:       b.ID,
121			DiffBase:     baseRev,
122			DiffHead:     resultRev,
123			ProjectRoot:  projectRoot,
124			EvidenceDir:  checksEvidence,
125			RunID:        newPreMergeRunID(),
126			BeadLabels:   b.Labels,
127			ChangedPaths: changed,
128		}
129	
130		results, err := checks.Run(ctx, toRun, ictx)
131		if err != nil {
132			return nil, fmt.Errorf("pre-merge checks: run: %w", err)
133		}
134	
135		outcome := &PreMergeChecksOutcome{
136			Results:     results,
137			Bypassed:    honouredBypass,
138			EvidenceDir: checksEvidence,
139			AttemptID:   filepath.Base(evidenceDir),
140			BaseRev:     baseRev,
141			ResultRev:   resultRev,
142		}
143		for _, r := range results {
144			if r.Status == checks.StatusBlock || r.Status == checks.StatusError {
145				outcome.Blocked = true
146				outcome.BlockingNames = append(outcome.BlockingNames, r.Name)
147			}
148		}

1	// Package metric defines the domain types and store for metric artifact
2	// observation. HistoryRecord, TrendSummary, ComparisonResult, Definition, and
3	// Thresholds are used by store.go and exec_bridge.go within this package and
4	// exported for use by cmd/metric.go. They are NOT projection-only types
5	// and cannot be inlined into cmd without creating a circular import.
6	package metric
7	
8	import "time"
9	
10	const (
11		ComparisonLowerIsBetter  = "lower-is-better"
12		ComparisonHigherIsBetter = "higher-is-better"
13		StatusPass               = "pass"
14		StatusFail               = "fail"
15	)
16	
17	// Definition describes an executable metric runtime record.
18	type Definition struct {
19		DefinitionID string            `json:"definition_id"`
20		MetricID     string            `json:"metric_id"`
21		Command      []string          `json:"command"`
22		Cwd          string            `json:"cwd,omitempty"`
23		Env          map[string]string `json:"env,omitempty"`
24		Thresholds   Thresholds        `json:"thresholds,omitempty"`
25		Comparison   string            `json:"comparison,omitempty"`
26		Active       bool              `json:"active"`
27		CreatedAt    time.Time         `json:"created_at"`
28	}
29	
30	// Thresholds describe the target values used for pass/fail evaluation.
31	type Thresholds struct {
32		Warn    float64 `json:"warn,omitempty"`
33		Ratchet float64 `json:"ratchet,omitempty"`
34		Unit    string  `json:"unit,omitempty"`
35	}
36	
37	// HistoryRecord stores one metric observation.
38	type HistoryRecord struct {
39		RunID        string           `json:"run_id"`
40		MetricID     string           `json:"metric_id"`
41		DefinitionID string           `json:"definition_id"`
42		ObservedAt   time.Time        `json:"observed_at"`
43		Status       string           `json:"status"`
44		Value        float64          `json:"value"`
45		Unit         string           `json:"unit,omitempty"`
46		Comparison   ComparisonResult `json:"comparison"`
47		ExitCode     int              `json:"exit_code"`
48		DurationMS   int64            `json:"duration_ms,omitempty"`
49		Stdout       string           `json:"stdout,omitempty"`
50		Stderr       string           `json:"stderr,omitempty"`
51		ArtifactID   string           `json:"artifact_id,omitempty"`
52	}
53	
54	// HistoryGroup groups one metric history series by unit.
55	type HistoryGroup struct {
56		Unit    string          `json:"unit,omitempty"`
57		Records []HistoryRecord `json:"records"`
58	}
59	
60	// ComparisonResult records the comparison against a baseline or target.
61	type ComparisonResult struct {
62		Baseline  float64 `json:"baseline"`
63		Delta     float64 `json:"delta"`
64		Direction string  `json:"direction"`
65	}
66	
67	// TrendSummary aggregates a sequence of history records.
68	type TrendSummary struct {
69		MetricID  string    `json:"metric_id"`
70		Count     int       `json:"count"`
71		Min       float64   `json:"min"`
72		Max       float64   `json:"max"`
73		Average   float64   `json:"average"`
74		Latest    float64   `json:"latest"`
75		Unit      string    `json:"unit,omitempty"`
76		UpdatedAt time.Time `json:"updated_at"`
77	}

1	package checks
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"encoding/json"
7		"fmt"
8		"os"
9		"os/exec"
10		"path/filepath"
11		"sort"
12		"sync"
13	)
14	
15	// Run evaluates every check in `all` whose AppliesTo filter matches `ctx`,
16	// in parallel, and returns the collected results sorted by check name.
17	//
18	// Each applicable check is run as `sh -c <command>` with the documented
19	// environment variables injected. After execution, the runner reads the
20	// expected ${EvidenceDir}/${name}.json file and parses it into a Result.
21	//
22	// Failure modes that produce status=error:
23	//   - Non-zero process exit.
24	//   - Result file missing.
25	//   - Result file unparseable / unknown status value.
26	//
27	// Run returns an error only for setup failures (e.g. cannot create
28	// EvidenceDir). Per-check failures are reported as Result{Status:error}
29	// in the returned slice.
30	func Run(ctx context.Context, all []Check, ictx InvocationContext) ([]Result, error) {
31		if err := os.MkdirAll(ictx.EvidenceDir, 0o755); err != nil {
32			return nil, fmt.Errorf("checks: create evidence dir: %w", err)
33		}
34	
35		var applicable []Check
36		for _, c := range all {
37			if c.Applies(ictx) {
38				applicable = append(applicable, c)
39			}
40		}
41	
42		results := make([]Result, len(applicable))
43		var wg sync.WaitGroup
44		for i := range applicable {
45			wg.Add(1)
46			go func(i int) {
47				defer wg.Done()
48				results[i] = runOne(ctx, applicable[i], ictx)
49			}(i)
50		}
51		wg.Wait()
52	
53		sort.Slice(results, func(i, j int) bool { return results[i].Name < results[j].Name })
54		return results, nil
55	}
56	
57	func runOne(ctx context.Context, c Check, ictx InvocationContext) Result {
58		resultPath := filepath.Join(ictx.EvidenceDir, c.Name+".json")
59		// Best-effort cleanup of any stale result file from a prior run so a
60		// check that crashes without writing one is correctly classified as
61		// status=error rather than silently picking up the previous output.
62		_ = os.Remove(resultPath)
63	
64		cmd := exec.CommandContext(ctx, "sh", "-c", c.Command)
65		cmd.Dir = ictx.ProjectRoot
66		cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(),
67			"BEAD_ID="+ictx.BeadID,
68			"DIFF_BASE="+ictx.DiffBase,
69			"DIFF_HEAD="+ictx.DiffHead,
70			"PROJECT_ROOT="+ictx.ProjectRoot,
71			"EVIDENCE_DIR="+ictx.EvidenceDir,
72			"RUN_ID="+ictx.RunID,
73			"CHECK_NAME="+c.Name,
74		)
75		var stderr bytes.Buffer
76		cmd.Stderr = &stderr
77	
78		runErr := cmd.Run()
79		exitCode := 0
80		if runErr != nil {
81			if ee, ok := runErr.(*exec.ExitError); ok {
82				exitCode = ee.ExitCode()
83			} else {
84				return Result{
85					Name:     c.Name,
86					Status:   StatusError,
87					Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q failed to start: %v", c.Name, runErr),
88					ExitCode: -1,
89					Stderr:   stderr.String(),
90				}
91			}
92		}
93	
94		if runErr != nil {
95			return Result{
96				Name:     c.Name,
97				Status:   StatusError,
98				Message:  fmt.Sprintf("check %q exited %d: %s", c.Name, exitCode, trim(stderr.String())),
99				ExitCode: exitCode,
100				Stderr:   stderr.String(),

Perfect! I now have all the requested file contents. Here's a comprehensive summary of RunID usage across the codebase:

---

## RunID Usage Summary

### 1. **cli/internal/exec/bead_runtime.go** (lines 295-500)

**Key Functions:**
- `runBead(rec RunRecord)` — converts a RunRecord to a bead using `rec.RunID` as the bead ID (line 303, 308)
- `runRecordFromBead(b bead.Bead)` — loads a RunRecord, assigns manifest.RunID from b.ID if empty (line 330)
- `readRunResult(manifest RunManifest)` — reads result.json from a path derived from `manifest.RunID` (line 366)
- `saveRunRecord(rec RunRecord)` — requires `rec.RunID` to be non-empty (line 407), creates directories with runID in the path (line 455)
- `runBundleDir(runID string)` — builds the bundle directory path using runID (line 482-483)
- `runAttachmentRef(workingDir, runID, name string)` — creates attachment references for stdout/stderr/result.json keyed by runID (line 486-487)
- `runAttachmentRefs(workingDir, runID string)` — returns a map of attachment references for a runID (line 490-495)

**Pattern:** RunID is used as the filesystem directory key under `.ddx/exec/runs/<runID>/`.

---

### 2. **cli/internal/exec/reachability.go** (full file)

**Key Functions:**
- `keepExecReachability()` — keepalive function that calls `store.Run()` and uses `rec.RunID` in subsequent calls to `store.Log()` and `store.Result()` (lines 65-71)

**Pattern:** Demonstrates the API contract where RunID is returned from `Run()` and used with `Log()` and `Result()`.

---

### 3. **cli/internal/metric/store.go** (lines 215-240)

**selectComparisonTarget() function:**
```go
for _, rec := range history {
    if rec.RunID == against {
        return rec, nil
    }
}
```
**Pattern:** RunID is used as a lookup key in history comparisons (line 226).

---

### 4. **cli/internal/metric/exec_bridge.go** (full file)

**Key Functions:**
- `metricHistoryFromExec(rec ddxexec.RunRecord)` — maps exec RunRecords to metric HistoryRecords, preserving `rec.RunID` as the metric HistoryRecord's RunID (line 29)

**Pattern:** RunID flows through the exec→metric adapter layer unchanged.

---

### 5. **cli/internal/metric/reachability.go** (full file)

**keepMetricReachability() function:**
- Creates a test RunManifest with `RunID: "metric-keepalive-run"` (line 74)
- Calls `metricHistoryFromExec()` with this RunRecord (line 72-94)
- Calls `store.Compare(artifactID, "baseline")` to exercise comparison logic (line 101)

**Pattern:** RunID is used as a stable identifier in metric history records.

---

### 6. **cli/cmd/exec.go** (lines 185-285)

**Key Command Handlers:**
- `newExecRunCommand()` — displays `rec.RunID` as the first field in output (line 198): `fmt.Fprintf(..., "%s  %s  %d\n", rec.RunID, rec.Status, rec.ExitCode)`
- `newExecLogCommand()` — takes `<run-id>` as CLI argument, passes to `store.Log(args[0])` (line 212)
- `newExecResultCommand()` — takes `<run-id>` as CLI argument, passes to `store.Result(args[0])` (line 240)
- `newExecHistoryCommand()` — displays `rec.RunID` for each history record (line 269)

**Pattern:** RunID is the primary user-facing identifier for accessing run data; displayed in output format.

---

### 7. **cli/cmd/metric.go** (lines 185-260)

**Key Command Handlers:**
- `runMetricRunCommand()` — displays `rec.RunID` in output (line 208)
- `runMetricCompareCommand()` — displays `rec.RunID` in comparison output (line 226)
- `runMetricHistoryCommand()` — displays `rec.RunID` for each history record (line 247)

**Pattern:** RunID is displayed as a key identifier in metric command output.

---

### 8. **cli/cmd/artifact.go** (lines 60-90)

**artifactGenerateCommand():**
```go
fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "%s  %s  %d\n", rec.RunID, rec.Status, rec.ExitCode)
```
**Pattern:** RunID is displayed in the artifact generation output (line 72).

---

### 9. **cli/internal/server/state_runs.go** (full file)

**Key Data Structures & Functions:**
- `runRecord` struct — has field `ID string` (not RunID) — this is the GraphQL-facing model (line 20)
- `synthesizeRunsFromBundles()` — creates Run.ID = `"exec-" + name` where name is the bundle ID (line 243)
- `synthesizeRunsFromSessions()` — creates Run.ID = `e.ID` (the session ID) (line 328)
- `GetRunGraphQL(id string)` — searches for runs by their ID field, calls `loadRunDetail(r)` (line 519-521)

**Pattern:** In the GraphQL layer, the run identifier is called `ID` (not RunID). Synthesized try-layer runs use `"exec-<bundleID>"` prefix.

---

### 10. **cli/internal/server/state_runs_detail.go** (full file)

**Key Functions:**
- `runBundleRoot(runID string)` — maps a RunID to its bundle directory (line 39-61)
- `bundleIDForRunID(projectRoot, runID string)` — resolves RunID to bundle ID (line 70-81):
  - If runID starts with `"exec-"`, strips that prefix
  - Otherwise, looks up the run-layer session entry via `agent.FindSessionIndex(logDir, runID)`
- `loadRunDetail(run *ddxgraphql.Run)` — enriches a Run with bundle files and prompt/response bodies using `run.ID` (line 254)

**Pattern:** RunID (or run.ID in GraphQL) is used to locate the execution bundle and load detail bodies.

---

### 11. **cli/internal/server/graphql_adapters.go** (full file)

**execLogAdapter:**
```go
func (a *execLogAdapter) GetExecLog(workingDir, runID string) (string, string, error) {
    store := ddxexec.NewStore(workingDir)
    return store.Log(runID)
}
```
**Pattern:** The adapter receives workingDir and runID separately; creates a store and calls `Log(runID)`.

---

### 12. **cli/internal/server/graphql_backfill_test.go** (lines 560-600)

**Test Assertions:**
```go
if resp3.Data.ExecRuns.Edges[0].Node.ID != rec.RunID {
    t.Errorf("execRuns: expected id=%s, got %q", rec.RunID, resp3.Data.ExecRuns.Edges[0].Node.ID)
}
// ...
if resp4.Data.ExecRun.ID != rec.RunID {
    t.Errorf("execRun(id): expected id=%s, got %q", rec.RunID, resp4.Data.ExecRun.ID)
}
```
**Pattern:** GraphQL query responses use `.ID` field, which should match the exec store's `rec.RunID`.

---

## Cross-Layer Summary

| Layer | Field Name | Context |
|-------|-----------|---------|
| **Exec Store** | `RunID` | Field on `RunRecord` struct; used for filesystem paths and API contracts |
| **Metric Adapter** | `RunID` | Preserved from exec layer via `metricHistoryFromExec()` |
| **CLI Commands** | `RunID` | User-facing identifier; displayed in output and used as command argument |
| **GraphQL/Server** | `ID` | Field on `Run` struct in GraphQL schema; mapped from exec store's RunID |
| **File System** | `runID` | Used as directory name in `.ddx/exec/runs/<runID>/` |

The key architectural insight: **RunID is the primary identifier throughout the system, but it's renamed to `ID` at the GraphQL layer** (lines 68, 328 in state_runs.go; line 254 in state_runs_detail.go).

14	// newGateCtxRepo initializes a real git repo and writes a single tracked file
15	// so HEAD has a real SHA the helper can pin / check out.
16	func newGateCtxRepo(t *testing.T) (root, headSHA string) {
17		t.Helper()
18		root = t.TempDir()
19		runGitInteg(t, root, "init", "-b", "main")
20		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.email", "test@ddx.test")
21		runGitInteg(t, root, "config", "user.name", "DDx Test")
22		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(root, "seed.txt"), []byte("seed\n"), 0644))
23		runGitInteg(t, root, "add", ".")
24		runGitInteg(t, root, "commit", "-m", "chore: initial seed")
25		headSHA = runGitInteg(t, root, "rev-parse", "HEAD")
26		return root, headSHA
27	}

29	// writeManifest serializes a minimal manifest with the given governing IDs to
30	// projectRoot/.ddx/executions/<attempt>/manifest.json and returns the
31	// bundle-relative manifest path the way ExecuteBead would set it on the result.
32	func writeManifest(t *testing.T, projectRoot, attemptID string, governingIDs []string) string {
33		t.Helper()
34		dirRel := filepath.Join(".ddx", "executions", attemptID)
35		dirAbs := filepath.Join(projectRoot, dirRel)
36		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(dirAbs, 0o755))
37	
38		type govRef struct {
39			ID string `json:"id"`
40		}
41		body := map[string]any{
42			"attempt_id": attemptID,
43			"bead_id":    "ddx-gatectx-bead",
44		}
45		if len(governingIDs) > 0 {
46			refs := make([]govRef, 0, len(governingIDs))
47			for _, id := range governingIDs {
48				refs = append(refs, govRef{ID: id})
49			}
50			body["governing"] = refs
51		}
52		raw, err := json.MarshalIndent(body, "", "  ")
53		require.NoError(t, err)
54		manifestRel := filepath.Join(dirRel, "manifest.json")
55		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(projectRoot, manifestRel), raw, 0o644))
56		return manifestRel
57	}

59	// resolveRef returns the SHA the ref points at, or "" when missing.
60	func resolveRef(t *testing.T, root, ref string) string {
61		t.Helper()
62		out, err := runGitIntegOutput(root, "rev-parse", "--verify", ref)
63		if err != nil {
64			return ""
65		}
66		return strings.TrimSpace(out)
67	}

399	func TestHistoryFallsBackToLegacyExecBundle(t *testing.T) {
400		wd := t.TempDir()
401		legacyRunDir := filepath.Join(wd, ".ddx", "exec", "runs", "exec-metric-startup-time@legacy")
402		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(legacyRunDir, 0o755))
403		manifest := RunManifest{
404			RunID:        "exec-metric-startup-time@legacy",
405			DefinitionID: "exec-metric-startup-time@legacy",
406			ArtifactIDs:  []string{"MET-001"},
407			StartedAt:    mustExecTime(t, "2026-04-03T15:00:00Z"),
408			FinishedAt:   mustExecTime(t, "2026-04-03T15:00:01Z"),
409			Status:       StatusSuccess,
410			ExitCode:     0,
411			Attachments: map[string]string{
412				"stdout": "stdout.log",
413				"stderr": "stderr.log",
414				"result": "result.json",
415			},
416		}
417		result := RunResult{Stdout: "legacy stdout", Stderr: "", Parsed: true, Value: 12.3, Unit: "ms"}
418		manifestRaw, err := json.MarshalIndent(manifest, "", "  ")
419		require.NoError(t, err)
420		resultRaw, err := json.MarshalIndent(result, "", "  ")
421		require.NoError(t, err)
422		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(legacyRunDir, "manifest.json"), manifestRaw, 0o644))
423		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(legacyRunDir, "result.json"), resultRaw, 0o644))
424		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(legacyRunDir, "stdout.log"), []byte(result.Stdout), 0o644))
425		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(legacyRunDir, "stderr.log"), []byte(result.Stderr), 0o644))
426	
427		store := NewStore(wd)
428		history, err := store.History("MET-001", "")
429		require.NoError(t, err)
430		require.Len(t, history, 1)
431		assert.Equal(t, manifest.RunID, history[0].RunID)
432		stdout, stderr, err := store.Log(manifest.RunID)
433		require.NoError(t, err)
434		assert.Equal(t, result.Stdout, stdout)
435		assert.Equal(t, result.Stderr, stderr)
436	}

String: func (s *Store) loadRunByID(runID string) (RunRecord, error) {
	if s.RunCollection != nil {
		beads, err := s.RunCollection.ReadAll()
		if err != nil {
			return RunRecord{}, err
		}
		for _, entry := range beads {
			if entry.ID != runID {
				continue
			}
			return s.runRecordFromBead(entry)
		}
	}
	return s.readRunBundle(filepath.Join(s.RunsDir, runID))

81	## Layer Extensions
82	
83	### Layer 1: invocation atom
84	
85	Layer 1 stores the DDx request envelope and the agent's typed response fields:
86	
87	- prompt attachment or prompt reference
88	- requested `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower`
89	- opaque passthrough constraints (`harness`, `provider`, `model`) when supplied
90	- non-routing work facts such as permissions, effort, timeout, and artifact id
91	- actual model and actual power returned by the agent
92	- token/cost/duration metadata
93	- upstream session id and session-log attachment pointer
94	- structured result attachment
95	
96	DDx does not persist route decisions as DDx-owned policy. Concrete
97	harness/provider/model values are recorded only as requested passthrough or
98	agent-reported facts.
99	
100	### Layer 2: bead attempt
101

106	- child layer-1 run ids
107	- commit/result revision, if produced

360	| `decompose_applied` — the decomposer agent filed 2–5 child beads; parent is left open but not execution-eligible until children are closed | `in_progress → open` with dependency edges to children filed; parent `execution-eligible=false` | add `decomposed` label | `decompose-applied` with `child_ids` list and `reframer_cost` in body | `Extra["children"]` lists child IDs and AC mapping; `Extra["execution-eligible"]=false`; `Extra["last-recovery"]` updated |
361	| `auto_recovery_failed` — both reframe and decompose attempts failed or the operator label `recovery:manual` was set; bead requires operator action | `in_progress → proposed` | add `triage:auto-recovery-failed` label | `auto-recovery-failed` with `reframe_attempt_cost` and `decompose_attempt_cost` (0 if not attempted) in body; `review-manual-required` is NOT fired here — this is a recovery, not a review | `Extra["last-recovery"]` records both attempt costs and failure reasons; DO NOT set `execute-loop-retry-after` |
362	| `per_bead_budget_exhausted` — cumulative per-bead cost for this bead exceeded the configured cap (`escalation.per_bead_budget_usd`) | `in_progress → open` (claim released; bead is re-claimable — budget exhaustion is recheckable, not terminal) | (none) | `per-bead-budget-exhausted` with `total_cost` in body | `Extra["last-run"]` only; DO NOT set `execute-loop-retry-after` — budget exhaustion requires operator action or config change, not time elapsed; see ADR-024 Per-Bead Budget |
363	
364	Legacy/backcompat `needs_human` and `triage:needs-investigation` labels are not
365	lifecycle controls. New routing uses `status=proposed` for operator decisions;
366	those labels may remain only as migration metadata until cleanup removes them.
367	
368	## 6. Claim Semantics

refactor(vocab): rename run-id → attempt-id across code, dirs, events, specs

Systematically renames all runID/run_id/run-id/RUN_ID identifiers to
attemptID/attempt_id/attempt-id/ATTEMPT_ID throughout cli/ and TD-031.

bead/) and TestExecutionDirs_AcceptBothNames (verifies cleanup scanner
counts both new and legacy-named execution dirs).

EOF
)" 2>&1
